title: heterotheca villosa var. nana
intro: Heterotheca villosa var. nana, also known as the dwarf yellow coneflower or dwarf hairy false goldeneye, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Heterotheca villosa var. nana are believed to offer various health benefits, including relief from colds, fever, and digestive issues, as well as wound healing and skin protection. The plant can be prepared through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and poultices for medicinal purposes.
